GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "How to keep your kids entertained inside according to a behavioural psychologist", "When your quarantine is less about home workouts and more likely filled with screaming kids, consider this your best option to #KeepPlaying with everyone happy.", "There's no denying that staying inside with children can be difficult, even at the best of times. Throw a global pandemic and social distancing measures into the mix and you've got yourself an unfavourable equation. And no matter how much screen time you let slip in, it really does feel like kids are never fully entertained – which is why parents all across the country are jumping on every resource out there to maximise their children's time at home. Enter Mattel, the creator of some of the world's most iconic characters and toys, including Barbie and Hot Wheels, and its recently launched solution: Mattel Playroom. Designed to be a space where the entire family can go for entertainment, the virtual living room has been set up with frustrated parents at front of mind – and there's plenty to explore. "Inside the Mattel Playroom and across our digital channels using #KeepPlaying, parents and caregivers have full access to activities, tips and inspiration, along with printable colouring pages and activities, free and ready-to-play games, crafting and DIY projects, animated, stop-motion and live-action videos and downloadable apps to be shared with kids," explains Monica Dreger, vice president of global consumer insights at Mattel. As a behavioural psychologist, Dreger knows the importance of keeping kids engaged and busy, especially in such an uncertain time. "Children are more aware of what is happening than you may think and are seeing their parents deal with many stressors, whether these be how to get the groceries they need, the possible loss of a job, or learning of a loved one who is sick," she says. The Mattel Playroom activities provide everything from learning-focused games to creative craft projects, perfect for the weekends or play time. Plus, with branding across the Mattel characters, there's a lot more incentive to get the kids interested in the first place. Colouring in Barbie is certainly a lot more fun than regular colouring! Dreger encourages parents to try and stick to a daily routine, where possible. That way, play time is consistent and something they can look forward to. Variety may be hard to find after weeks of the same games, but Dreger says there are ways of making the same games seem new – it's just about getting creative. "Keeping kids entertained can feel like a full-time job, but remember that boredom can create the perfect state of mind for innovation and creativity. If you've played all of the games you have at home a thousand times already, challenge everyone to create a new rule or way to play a game. For example, play Uno in a new way by coming up with a storyline for each card, like Green 3, and each player can continue the story." Access to the Mattel Playroom is free, and parents can access it online. With updated games and advice every week, including how to celebrate special occasions in lockdown and dedicated YouTube playlists to watch with the kids, there's plenty to explore. And let's be honest, at a time like this, no one's going to judge you for sitting your kids in front of the television for three hours straight. If it means you get some work done, or gives you time to watch the next episode of your favourite show, we're on board. You can shop Mattel's iconic portfolio of brands, including BarbieВ®, Fisher-PriceВ®, Hot WheelsВ®, Thomas & FriendsВ® and more, at Big W, Kmart, Target and all good toy retailers."
